name: Starobinsky R + R^2 inflation
summary: Adds a curvature-squared term to the Einstein-Hilbert action: S = (M_Pl^2/2) integral d^4x sqrt(-g) (R + R^2/(6 M^2)). At high curvature the R^2 term dominates and drives an inflationary de Sitter epoch; quantum-induced trace anomaly contributions provided the original motivation. Reformulated in the Einstein frame, the model becomes a single-field inflaton with a specific plateau potential.

addresses_puzzles.note: Predicts n_s = 1 - 2/N ~ 0.965 and r = 12/N^2 ~ 0.003 for N ~ 55 e-folds, in excellent agreement with Planck + BICEP/Keck and squarely within reach of CMB-S4 and LiteBIRD.

touches_benchmarks.note: r ~ 12/N^2 ~ 0.003 for N=55; below current upper bound r < 0.036 but above LiteBIRD / CMB-S4 sensitivity ~ 0.001. Discovering r in this range is the cleanest test.
Predicts n_s ~ 0.965 in the observed range and a specific running of the spectral index alpha_s ~ -2/N^2.

new_fields: effective scalar (the scalaron) from the R^2 term in the Einstein frame
introduces.note: Single new dimensional parameter M. The number of e-folds N is set by post-inflation reheating physics.

history: Starobinsky (1980), 'A new type of isotropic cosmological models without singularity', Phys. Lett. B 91, 99-102 — predates Guth's inflation framework but is often retroactively counted as the first inflationary model. Re-popularised after Planck 2013 ruled out simpler chaotic-inflation models with V ~ phi^n; Starobinsky's predictions sit at the centre of the observed (n_s, r) plane.

references.citation: Starobinsky, 'A new type of isotropic cosmological models without singularity', Phys. Lett. B 91, 99 (1980)
Mukhanov and Chibisov, 'Quantum fluctuations and a nonsingular universe', JETP Lett. 33, 532 (1981)
